title: Strait of Hormuz closure sparks revised aluminium price forecast, pushing it above $4000/t
summary: ING Group has projected aluminum prices to exceed USD 4000 per tonne due to escalating geopolitical conflict in the Middle East, particularly the potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This revision reflects concerns over supply disruptions from key producing regions.

title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
summary: Aluminum prices on the LME have surged past $3,000 per tonne, reaching as high as $3,220, driven by supply deficits, demand from the energy transition sector, China's production caps, and carbon-border taxes. Western low-carbon producers like Alcoa are benefiting, while downstream industries face higher costs.

title: After US tariffs, aluminium price surge due to Middle East conflict causing concern to Coca-Cola

title: Aluminum Supply Crisis: Energy Constraints & Trade Impact

summary: LME aluminum prices have outperformed copper, reaching $3,546.50 per ton amid a supply crunch from Middle East smelter closures due to the war in Iran and gas supply disruptions. summary: Aluminum prices surged 1.7% on the LME due to Middle East conflict disrupting 9% of global production outside China, with inventory withdrawals doubling. Potential Strait of Hormuz issues could raise prices and disrupt supply chains.

title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs from Middle East conflict closing the Strait of Hormuz, impacting shipments to the U.S. Supply concerns have flipped the market contango into a $47.34 per ton premium. Regional production disruptions exacerbate global tightness.

title: Century Aluminum Joins EGA Project to Build First U.S. Smelter in Almost 50 Years
summary: Century Aluminum partners with Emirates Global Aluminum on a new smelter in Inola, Oklahoma, the first U.S. facility in nearly 50 years. The plant will produce 750,000 tonnes of aluminum annually, enhancing domestic capacity. This addresses growing supply needs amid global constraints.

summary: Recent developments in recycled aluminium and sustainability include plant fire disruptions, low-carbon cable trials, recycling gains, and strategic global expansions. These updates shape the aluminium industry's push towards sustainable practices.

summary: Metal prices fell sharply on the London and Shanghai exchanges today due to trade tensions. Copper and steel experienced significant declines, while silver and platinum prices rose. This reflects ongoing volatility in global metal markets amid geopolitical pressures.

title: Raw steel production ticks higher, nears multi-year high
summary: US domestic raw steel production rose to 1,781,000 short tons for the week ending March 21, up 0.4% from the prior week, approaching the four-year high of 1,817,000 tons from mid-February. Year-to-date production reached 20,394,000 tons, 4.9% higher than the same period in 2025, with mill capability utilization at 77.0%. Production increased in the Midwest and South regions but declined in others.

title: US raw steel production increases by 0.4 percent - week 12, 2026
summary: US raw steel production for the week ending March 21, 2026, was 1.781 million net tons, up 0.4% from 1.774 million net tons the previous week, with capacity utilization at 77.0%. This marks a 4.6% increase from the same week in 2025. Adjusted year-to-date production through March 21 was 20.394 million net tons, up 4.9% from last year at 77.2% utilization.

title: Copper prices drop amid global growth fears, plump stocks
summary: Copper prices fell 9.4% to $12,081.74/metric ton as of March 23 due to fears of weak economic growth triggered by the US-Israel war with Iran and high energy prices. Warehouse stocks are growing after a period of tightness, adding downward pressure on prices. BMO Capital Markets downgraded their near-term copper outlook, citing disruption to the previously constructive macro setup for 2026.

title: Copper price jumps as Trump delays strikes on Iranian energy assets
summary: Copper prices rebounded 2% to $12,167 per metric ton after US President Donald Trump postponed planned strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days. The temporary de-escalation signal calmed global markets and reduced fears of deeper supply shocks. Falling inventories in China, down 78,700 tons to 486,200 tons, also provided support to the market.

title: Copper Drops $0.19 | Scrap Metal Prices Mixed
summary: Copper led scrap metal market declines, falling $0.19 per pound across major grades. LME 3-month copper closed at $12,395.50 per ton, down 2.97%, pressured by a stronger US dollar and demand concerns tied to rising energy costs and geopolitical disruptions.

title: BHP Insights: how copper will shape our future
summary: BHP discusses copper's essential role in the energy transition and digitalization, projecting global copper demand will grow by around 70% to over 50 million tonnes annually by 2050. The article highlights technology solutions including AI-enabled insights in processing and new leaching technologies to address the looming copper supply challenge, though widespread impact is not expected until the mid-2030s.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: This report analyzes the global outlook for copper supply and demand through 2040, emphasizing copper's critical role in electrification and renewable energy systems. title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: US copper prices are expected to remain volatile in 2026 due to multiple factors including Chinese demand, copper supply dynamics, and AI sector demand. Market participants are bullish on 2026 prospects, though there is disagreement about whether the copper market will enter a supply deficit, with all participants forecasting an approaching deficit based on lack of new mine commissions and declining South American output.

title: Hanrui says Indonesian nickel matte smelter on track for March 2026 start-up
summary: Chinese cobalt producer Nanjing Hanrui Cobalt Co Ltd announced that its high-grade nickel matte smelter in Indonesia remains on track for a March 2026 start-up. The update confirms progress in the project's development amid ongoing nickel production expansions in the region.

title: This ASX mining stock just jumped. Here's what's driving the move today
summary: Nickel Industries Ltd (ASX: NIC) shares rose 6.78% after securing approval for its 2026 nickel ore sales plan in Indonesia, known as the RKAB. The approval permits around 14.3 million wet metric tonnes of ore sales, up from 9 million previously, supporting increased output at its Hengjaya Mine and downstream projects.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: PT Weda Bay Nickel, operating the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been ordered to cut its 2026 ore production quota to 12 million tons from 42 million tons in 2025. The move aims to boost global nickel prices, with the company planning to seek a revision.

title: Indonesia to cut nickel mining quota in 2026
summary: Indonesia's energy ministry will reduce the 2026 nickel production quota to 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million tonnes in 2025. This cut, aimed at regulating rising nickel prices, falls short of estimated 330 million tonnes consumption, potentially leading to reduced smelter output or increased imports.

title: SMM Stainless Steel Flash: Indonesian Industry Warns of Ecosystem Disruptions from US Trade Pact and Mining Quota Cuts
summary: The Indonesian Nickel Industry Forum (FINI) warned of disruptions from US trade pacts and the government's cut of the 2026 nickel ore production quota to 260-270 million tonnes from 379 million previously. These measures could impact the nickel ecosystem amid ongoing supply adjustments.

title: Inside a $1.1B deal to reshore critical minerals refining
summary: Nth Cycle's startup has secured a $1.1 billion agreement with Trafigura to expand its Ohio facility, quadrupling capacity to process up to 12,400 metric tons of scrap using an innovative electrochemical system for refining nickel, cobalt, copper, and rare earths. This smaller, more efficient technology reduces capital expenditures compared to traditional refineries and addresses U.S. and Europe's dependency on Indonesia and China for nickel supply. The system processes black mass from recycled batteries and nickel catalysts from oil and gas, promoting sustainable refining.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries reported a perfect safety record with a 12-month Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate of 0.00 and no injuries in the quarter. The company advanced sustainability through a strategic acquisition of a 10% stake in Excelsior Nickel Cobalt, securing an offtake agreement for traceable nickel cathode products supplied to SpaceX. It also received national awards for ESG and governance in Indonesia.

title: Re-energising the nickel industry through sustainable battery production
summary: Queensland Pacific Metals is developing the TECH Project to produce high-grade, ethically sourced nickel using the patented DNi Process™ on imported laterite ore from New Caledonia. The facility aims to be carbon-negative, supporting sustainable production of nickel and cobalt for EV batteries with binding offtake agreements from LG Energy Solution and POSCO. This innovation positions QPM as a key supplier in the green energy transition.

title: Prysmian develops world's first cable with negative carbon footprint
summary: Prysmian announced the development of the world's first cable with a negative carbon footprint, using a cradle-to-gate approach where raw material procurement and manufacturing remove more CO2 than they generate.
